title: Building a Lightweight Markdown Parser
description: How the custom parser converts markdown to safe static HTML.
contentHtml:
The build script walks through the posts/ directory and generates full post pages.

Supported blocks

  1. Headings
  2. Paragraphs
  3. Lists
  4. Blockquotes
  5. Code blocks

Table support

FeatureSupported
HeadingsYes
TablesYes
LibrariesNo

The parser uses plain Node.js and no external dependencies.

title: Welcome to My Blog
description: A quick overview of how this dependency-free static blog works.
contentHtml:
This site is generated from Markdown into static HTML.

Why this approach

  • Fast pages
  • No client-side framework
  • Easy deployment on GitHub Pages

Syntax support

The parser supports bold, italic, and inline code.

Simple tools and clear structure usually age better.

Example code block

function greet(name) {
  return `Hello, ${name}!`;
}

Read the posts section to explore more posts.
